steps.title: Identify the clause boundaries
steps.content: The first independent clause ends at the blank: “In Norway, the Longyearbyen observatory site monitors activity in the upper atmosphere of the northern ______.” The second independent clause begins with “in Australia, another observatory site, Buckland Park, monitors the sky of the southern hemisphere.”

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A semicolon separates two independent clauses. Choice D correctly uses a semicolon to join the two independent clauses about the two observatory sites.

The other three options fail to properly separate the clauses—whether through missing punctuation, a comma alone (comma splice), or a misplaced conjunction.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
